trimmedChitChat.startTrimmed: 00:00:00-00:13:22 — Pre-lecture conversation: AAGL conference, MIGS/REI tubal surgery training, RRM apolitical positioning, $30B private equity industry, community connections, screen-share tech setup. All off-topic for FABM course.
trimmedChitChat.endTrimmed: 01:11:06-01:25:32 — Closing pleasantries, scheduling, brief visitor check-in.

lessons.title: Introduction to FABMs: Definition, Prevalence, and the Provider Knowledge Gap
lessons.topicSummary: Naomi introduces Dr. Nolan. Definition of FABMs (cervical mucus, BBT, urinary hormones, bleeding biomarkers). Rising prevalence (1% to 3%+ by 2018). Critique of misleading CDC 24% lump statistic. Provider knowledge gap: 40-50% of OBGYN trainees lack FABM training, 80-90% cannot explain specific methods, only 4% receive formal training.

lessons.title: The Six Categories of FABMs: Methods, Physiology, and Effectiveness Rates
lessons.topicSummary: Six FABM categories. Calendar methods. Cervical mucus-only (Billings, Creighton, two-day) with deep dive into Eric Odeblad's research. BBT (Natural Cycles, BioAlth). Symptothermal (Sensiplan at 1.8% typical use, approaching IUD effectiveness). Hormonal monitoring (Marquette, Persona). Symptohormonal combinations.

lessons.title: New Horizons: Using the Cycle for Wellness, Exercise, and Nutrition Optimization
lessons.topicSummary: Cycle as wellness tool. Stacy Sims/Huberman podcast reference. 6-month RCT showing cycle-tailored weight loss nearly doubled results (11.1 kg vs 6.8 kg). Estradiol stabilizes muscle membranes (follicular phase = strength training); luteal phase favors endurance and recovery.

lessons.title: Clinical Applications: PMS/PMDD, Cooperative Hormone Therapy, and PCOS
lessons.topicSummary: PMS/PMDD diagnosis via accurate symptom diaries. Cooperative hormone therapy (FABM data to supplement, not suppress). PCOS pathophysiology: hyperinsulinemia drives ovarian androgen production, follicular atresia. Hypothesis: limiting insulin in early follicular phase via low-glycemic diet, fiber, ACV, post-meal movement, or fasting may rescue follicles.

lessons.title: Q&A: Cervical Mucus as Primary Biomarker
lessons.topicSummary: Dr. Nolan's favorite-method reflection. Cervical mucus as 'king or queen' of fertility biomarkers. Partner-shared FABM literacy improving relationship communication around PMS/PMDD.

lessons.title: Q&A: Sleep, Cortisol, and Cycle-Phase Nutrition
lessons.topicSummary: Critique of exercise physiology research using OCP-suppressed college cohorts. AI-assisted personalized cycle-phase meal planning via ChatGPT. Skipping breakfast: morning cortisol spike, glucose mobilization, brain-ovary signaling disruption. Sleep quality and metabolism (6-hour sleepers consume ~300 extra cal/day).

lessons.title: Q&A: Comparing Marquette and Creighton Methods
lessons.topicSummary: Marquette vs Creighton method comparison. Cervical mucus observations are objective with validated training. LH and estrogen tests can be false-positive in PCOS and insulin resistance. Urinary hormone metabolites have hydration and timing lag issues. Closing principle: when using multiple markers, always defer to the most fertile sign.

notes: APPROVED 2026-05-01 with split option B then re-split. 7 lessons. Adjacency: 802->1513->2346->2794->3419->3690->4117->4266. Lesson 7 is short (2:29) but topically distinct.
